currentUse: Active Roman Catholic worship site — St. George Church, 443 Park Ave., Bridgeport CT, now operated jointly with (merged into) St. Charles Borromeo Parish, serving a predominantly Spanish-speaking congregation with Masses in Spanish and English
summary: St. George Church at 443 Park Avenue, Bridgeport — the 1907 Romanesque Revival church built for a Lithuanian congregation — is standing and remains an active Catholic worship site in the Diocese of Bridgeport, with a congregation Wikipedia describes as roughly 80 percent Spanish-speaking. It no longer functions as an independent parish: it is now operated jointly with St. Charles Borromeo Parish on Bridgeport's East Side, whose website presents the two as a single "Saint Charles & Saint George" parish serving over 4,500 immigrant families with Masses in Spanish, English, Brazilian Portuguese, and Haitian Creole. The current published Mass schedule at St. George is weekday Masses Monday, Tuesday, and Friday at 7:00 pm, a Saturday 6:30 pm Spanish vigil, and Sunday Masses at 8:30 am (English) and 10:30 am (Spanish) — no Lithuanian Mass appears on the official schedule. The pastor of the joint parish is Rev. J. Abelardo Vasquez, in office since January 2021, assisted by three parochial vicars; no clergy or staff member is identified with a Lithuanian ministry. The building's interior retains exceptionally rich Lithuanian fabric — Lithuanian donor inscriptions in the stained glass, a Lithuanian chapel-post, a 1913 Lithuanian-inscribed altar cross, and an Our Lady of Vilnius painting — per the Global True Lithuania heritage survey, which reports the church "still operating and, in fact, open most of the time." The Diocese of Bridgeport's own Mass-times directory no longer lists St. George as a separate Bridgeport parish entry (only St. Charles Borromeo appears), and third-party directories now point St. George's website to stcharlesbridgeport.org, consistent with the consolidation.

developments.headline: St. George operating jointly with St. Charles Borromeo Parish
developments.detail: By July 2023 the two East Side Bridgeport parishes were publishing a combined bulletin titled 'St. Charles Borromeo + St. George' (bulletin dated 2023-07-02 on ParishesOnline), and the St. Charles Borromeo website now presents them as a single 'Saint Charles & Saint George' parish with one office (391 Ogden St.), one pastor (Rev. J. Abelardo Vasquez), and a unified Mass schedule. Search-result snippets from the Diocese of Bridgeport site describe St. Charles Borromeo as having 'merged with St. George Parish in July' (2023), though the canonical decree itself could not be opened for verification.

sources.title: Saint Charles Borromeo Parish & St. George — parish website (home, parish information, clergy directory, Mass schedule pages)
History — Saint Charles Borromeo Parish

developments.headline: No Lithuanian Mass on the current official schedule
developments.detail: The joint parish's published Mass schedule for St. George (weekdays Mon/Tue/Fri 7:00 pm; Sat vigil 6:30 pm Spanish; Sun 8:30 am English and 10:30 am Spanish) lists no Lithuanian-language Mass, and the parish clergy/staff directory and ministries pages contain no Lithuanian ministry. One third-party Mass aggregator search result claimed a Lithuanian Mass on the 3rd Sunday of the month at 12 pm, but that page (catholicclocks.com) returned HTTP 403 and could not be opened to verify.

sources.title: Mass Schedule — St. Charles & St. George
Clergy and Staff Directory — St. Charles

developments.headline: Lithuanian interior fabric documented as intact
developments.detail: The Global True Lithuania survey of Connecticut Lithuanian heritage sites reports the Bridgeport St. George church 'still operating and, in fact, open most of the time,' now mostly Hispanic, with 'more Lithuanian details than in many churches of Lithuania': Lithuanian benefactor inscriptions in the stained glass, a Lithuanian chapel-post, the 1913 altar-cross inscription 'Misijos atmintis 1913', and an Our Lady of Vilnius painting. A 2018 site-author comment adds that 'the Lithuanian community still makes a distinct part of the parish' under an Argentine priest with mostly Latino parishioners.

sources.title: Connecticut: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage

developments.headline: St. George no longer listed separately in diocesan Mass-times directory
developments.detail: The Diocese of Bridgeport Mass Times directory lists eleven Bridgeport parishes including St. Charles Borromeo but not St. George, while the diocese's parish dropdown still carries a standalone 'St. George, Bridgeport' entry — an inconsistent directory state consistent with a recently merged worship site. The CT Catholic Cemeteries parish list still shows St. George at 443 Park Avenue but its website link resolves to stcharlesbridgeport.org.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: Building status "standing" and diocese_rc ownership are confirmed — the church is an active Diocese of Bridgeport worship site, and the majority-Spanish-speaking congregation and richly Lithuanian interior both match the archive notes. Two refinements/possible contradictions: (1) The archive says "Lithuanian Masses still held" (active 2023-24), but the joint parish's current official Mass schedule (fetched July 2026) lists no Lithuanian Mass, the diocese Mass-times directory lists no Lithuanian Mass anywhere, and no Lithuanian ministry appears in the parish staff/groups pages; one unverifiable aggregator listing (catholicclocks.com, HTTP 403) claimed a 3rd-Sunday 12 pm Lithuanian Mass. Lithuanian Masses may have ended or become occasional/unadvertised — the archive claim could not be confirmed for the present day. (2) The archive treats Šv. Jurgio as a surviving standalone parish; in fact it no longer operates independently — since around July 2023 it functions as one parish with St. Charles Borromeo ("Saint Charles & Saint George"), a consolidation the archive record does not capture. Also note Wikipedia names Fr. Andris Alexis Moronta as parish priest while the parish's own directory names Rev. J. Abelardo Vasquez as pastor since January 2021; the parish's own site should be preferred.
gaps: Could not verify: (1) whether any Lithuanian Mass (e.g., the 3rd-Sunday noon Mass claimed in a catholicclocks.com search snippet) still takes place — the page returned HTTP 403, the parish's own schedule omits it, and the litua.com Lithuanian church directory entry for Bridgeport is a blank/placeholder; a call to the parish (203-335-1797) would settle it. (2) The exact date and canonical form of the St. George–St. Charles Borromeo consolidation — search snippets from bridgeportdiocese.org say the parishes "merged in July" 2023 and a combined bulletin dated 2023-07-02 exists on ParishesOnline, but the diocesan decree/announcement page itself could not be opened (404s on the diocese archive pages; the bulletin PDF exceeded fetch size limits), so "merged vs. linked" and the precise date remain unconfirmed. (3) No press coverage, preservation campaign, sale listing, or reunion coverage for this church was found in searches (English and Lithuanian, 2024-2026). (4) Searches for "Šv. Jurgio Bridgeport" mostly surface the unrelated St. George Lithuanian parish in Chicago's Bridgeport neighborhood (demolished, now a parking lot) — care was taken not to conflate the two; all findings above were confirmed against the 443 Park Ave., Bridgeport CT address. (5) Restructuring exposure: the Diocese of Bridgeport has an active program of parish mergers/deanery reorganization, but no opened source states any further planned change affecting the St. George building specifically.
